title: Obama Presidential Library: Internal NSC Deliberations Regarding Neda Agha-Soltan - Puneet Talwar

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to all records, including but not limited to electronic mail (emails), formal memorandums, briefing papers, "Night Notes," Situation Room reports, and handwritten notes, that were drafted by, sent to, or received by Puneet Talwar between the dates of June 20, 2009, and June 30, 2009, which relate to the death of Neda Agha-Soltan and its impact on the "Green Movement" protests in Iran.

For context, the death of Neda Agha-Soltan was widely distributed online. Obama mentioned on June 23rd of 2009 that he had seen the video of her death and that the death was "heartbreaking."

Wikipedia entry: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_of_Neda_Agha-Soltan
Obama Press conference where he was asked about he death of Neda: 

II. Targeted Staff & Systems
To assist in your search, please prioritize the following locations and identifiers

Staff Member: Puneet Talwar (Special Assistant to the President and Senior Director for Iran, Iraq, and the Gulf States).

Collection: National Security Council (NSC) Records / Iran Directorate.

System of Arrangement: Please search the Exchange Email - Presidential search and access sets (SAS) and the NSC Staff Member Office Files (SMOF) for Puneet Talwar.

III. Potential Email Addresses/Identifiers
Based on White House naming conventions during the 2009-2017 period, I request that you include the following internal email formats in your search:

ptalwar@nsc.eop.gov
puneet_talwar@nsc.eop.gov
puneet.talwar@nsc.eop.gov
ptalwar@who.eop.gov

IV. Search Keywords/Terms
Please use the following terms to identify responsive records:

"Neda"
"Agha-Soltan"
"Soltani"
"Green Movement"
"June 20"
"YouTube video" / "Viral video"
"Heartbreaking" (specifically in reference to press conference preparation and/or the press conference aftermath)
"ندا آقასلطان"

If you deny any part of this request, please cite the specific exemption that justifies the refusal and provide a list of the withheld documents (a Vaughn Index).

Thank you for the Obama administration's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: NSC and EOP Communications on Twitter and Iranian Protests (June 2009)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: NSC and EOP Communications on Twitter and Iranian Protests (June 2009)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request access to all records from June 12, 2009 through June 30, 2009, with an extended search period through September 30, 2009 for retrospective discussions, concerning U.S. government interactions with Twitter, Inc. during the June 2009 Iranian "Iranian Green Movement" election protests. For context, the month of June 2009 was of peak importance, this time frame included the June 20th death of Neda Agha Soltan. 

Scope of Request

I request records within the Executive Office of the President (EOP), including the National Security Council (NSC), that reference:

Twitter, Inc.
Social media platforms in the context of the Iranian protests
Any U.S. government effort to influence, delay, or respond to Twitter service operations (including the widely reported June 15, 2009 maintenance delay request)

Key Individuals (for search purposes):

Jared Cohen (State Department Policy Planning Staff; liaison to technology sector) 
NSC staff responsible for Iran / Middle East, 
NSC staff responsible for technology, communications, or public diplomacy
Senior White House officials involved in public diplomacy or strategic communications

Please make a good faith effort to search for email addresses associated with Jared Cohen. Include the following possibilities: CohenJ@state.gov, Jared.Cohen@state.gov, JCohen@state.gov, CohenJA@state.gov

Note to FOIA Staff: Multiple reports in contemporaneous journalism (Foreign Policy, Time magazine, June 2009) confirm that Cohen used his State Department email — not a personal account — to contact Twitter. The email in question is specifically described as having been sent on June 15, 2009, afternoon Eastern time. Records from the State Department's OpenNet email archiving system, which automatically captured all sent and received emails per 5 FAM 443, should contain this record. I respectfully request that all probable address variants(including but not limited to the ones listed above) be searched.

Records Requested

Email and Electronic Communications -
All emails and attachments within EOP systems that reference:
Jared Cohen’s reported outreach to Twitter in June 2009
Discussions of Twitter’s operational status during the Iranian protests
Internal White House or NSC deliberations regarding social media and Iran

NSC and White House Internal Records -
All memoranda, briefing materials, situation reports, meeting notes, or internal communications referencing:
The Iranian election protests and digital communications platforms
Any coordination or awareness of State Department outreach to Twitter
Policy considerations regarding information flow, protest visibility, or communications strategy

Briefing and Decision-Making Materials -
All records prepared for or circulated among senior officials that reference:
Twitter’s role in the Iranian protests (including but not limited to the impact of the #IranElection, #Neda, #ندا, #GreenMovement, #WhereIsMyVote, #رای_من_کجاست hashtags)
U.S. policy toward social media platforms during this period
Any discussion of whether to encourage or discourage private sector action affecting communications in Iran

Post-Event Analysis -
Records dated July 1, 2009 through September 30, 2009 that evaluate, summarize, or reference:
The June 2009 Twitter-related communication
Lessons learned or policy discussions regarding digital platforms and foreign protests

Search Guidance (to Assist Archivists)

To facilitate retrieval, I recommend searching EOP and NSC electronic records using combinations of the following keywords:

“Twitter”
“Iran” OR “Iran election” OR “Iran protests” OR "#IranElection" 
"Neda Agah-Solton" OR "Neda Agah Solton" OR "Neda" or "ندا آقاسلطان"
“Cohen” OR "Jared Cohen" 
“maintenance” OR “outage”
“June 15”
“social media”

This request is intended to capture records maintained within EOP systems, including NSC email archives, shared drives, and briefing materials. I understand that some responsive records may remain restricted under the PRA and applicable access provisions.

If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. In plain English: there is value in seeing, line-by-line, what's being redacted and what exemptions are being claimed. 

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency, and I look forward to working together on this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Body-Worn Camera Footage, Arrest of Kamil J. Labudda, October 3, 2025

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503 et seq., I respectfully request copies of the following records:

All body-worn camera (BWC) footage captured by any officer(s) of the Pigeon Forge Police Department in connection with the traffic stop, detention, and/or arrest of Kamil Janucz Labudda (DOB on file) on or about October 3, 2025, at approximately 12:17 AM. The arrest resulted in a charge of reckless driving.

Additionally, I request copies of the following related records:

1. The incident report and/or arrest report associated with this arrest; 2. Any dashcam (in-car camera) footage from the patrol vehicle(s) involved; 3. The booking record, including the booking photograph.

I am a citizen of the United States. I understand that the TPRA grants the right of inspection to citizens of Tennessee; however, I respectfully request that the Department exercise its discretion to make these records available to me as a U.S. citizen, consistent with principles of government transparency.

I am willing to pay reasonable costs for the duplication and production of these records. Please provide me with an estimate of charges before proceeding, as is my right under Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503(a)(7)(C). I would prefer to receive the records electronically (via email attachment or secure download link) if possible.

If any portion of the requested records is withheld, I request a written explanation citing the specific legal exemption(s) that apply.

Please respond within seven (7) business days as required by Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503(a)(2)(B).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Dawid Dabrowski.
